title: Viscaria coeli-rosa
intro: Viscaria coeli-rosa, also known as Chinese gentian, is a plant species native to China that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Viscaria coeli-rosa include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Viscaria coeli-rosa is often used to make teas, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat specific health conditions.
taxonomy: Viscaria coeli-rosa is classified in the family Polemoniaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plant species. Viscaria coeli-rosa is a member of the genus Viscaria, which is a group of flowering plants native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The plant is often placed in the subfamily Polemonioideae. More specifically, Viscaria coeli-rosa is sometimes referred to as a member of the tribe Polemonieae.
uses: Viscaria coeli-rosa, also known as sky bellflower, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional folk medicine, Viscaria coeli-rosa is used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their antioxidant and cytotoxic activities, which could have implications for cancer treatment and prevention. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Viscaria coeli-rosa.
benefits: Viscaria coeli-rosa, also known as the sky rose or skybloom, is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. constituents: Viscaria coeli-rosa is known for its rich content of anthocyanins, which are responsible for its vibrant color. Additionally, it contains phenolic acids, including ferulic acid, which have antioxidant properties. Other active constituents of Viscaria coeli-rosa include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which contribute to its potential health benefits. parts: Viscaria coeli-rosa, also known as sky rose or alpine viscaria, is a flowering plant. Unfortunately, information about the medicinal parts of Viscaria coeli-rosa is limited.
